search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae post traumatic red eye guideline PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
General tetanus guidance was not treated as eye-trauma evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for corneal abrasion and listed urgent blunt, chemical, penetrating, foreign-body, hyphema, and laceration contexts; not exact for every ocular trauma subtype.
setting_applicability: Direct for first-contact eye triage; partial for DHA telehealth and in-person trauma examination.
UAE_applicability: DHA post-traumatic red-eye warnings are directly applicable in Dubai; College referral guidance requires UAE clinician review and local pathways.
recency_verification: College corneal-abrasion Version 15, current referral annex, and DHA Issue 2 were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official versions or DHA issue were identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped minor injury, foreign-body, contact-lens, pain, redness, blurred vision, photophobia, and abrasion fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped fluorescein and clinician-arranged referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped chemical, penetrating, hyphema, foreign-body, laceration, blunt-trauma, severe-abrasion, and urgent-referral warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped reduced vision, severe pain, diplopia, eye-movement, and pupil warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped chemical and mechanical insult context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped post-traumatic red eye, reduced acuity, fixed pupil, photophobia, and severe-pain warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped trauma, chemical, foreign-body, visual, conjunctival, corneal, and pupil documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped return and referral documentation already covered by primary sources.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Tetanus status, scratch terminology beyond abrasion, bleeding without a specified lesion, anterior-chamber depth, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, previous records, medication choices, and fixed one-to-two-day review remain unsupported.
No penetrating injury, hyphema, or other diagnosis may be inferred automatically.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
